title: Methods for Handling Deadlocks
description: A flowchart depicting the four primary strategies for dealing with deadlocks in an operating system, along with their sub-methods.
relationships: The four main methods (Prevention, Avoidance, Detection, Recovery) are distinct strategies. Prevention uses protocols to negate one of the four necessary conditions. Avoidance requires knowledge of future resource requests to dynamically avoid unsafe states. Detection employs algorithms to identify a deadlock state. Recovery is the action taken after detection to break the deadlock. The Ignore method is a valid fifth strategy.
